what_it_does: This file is an evidence-classed interpretive assessment of the gallery front-interface system, summarizing verified findings about the gallery's spatial shell, phone-archetype UI, 49-piece mockup inventory, gaps, and the first-slice build seam that wires projection drill-in events into a live-composed unit renderer.
observations: The gallery is a spatial shell with one canvas and sliding rooms; the gallery itself is order-1 with bench, map, and almanac as overlays.
Mobile form is refined (narrow breakpoint 920px, scrubber, sheet, scale anchor) but no true desktop form exists yet.
There are 49 distinct pieces and 74 version files in the mockup manifest, grouped into Slides, Application, and Further Out.
All 19 Application pieces use the shared portrait phone archetype defined in ui/phone.css and ui/piece.css.
Mockup files are small and consume live DNA tokens and shared CSS/JS organisms, so the look already resolves from DNA; only structure and content remain baked.
A projection:select CustomEvent contract passes a drill address to the gallery; the gallery's downstream half is renderGallery(address).
Increment 1 built DNA.renderUnit, DNA.unitFromCorpus, DNA.unitFromStory, and hubNetwork, proving one renderer with many source adapters.
The renderer went through a visible iteration trajectory v3 (text-wall) → v6 (field) → v7 (connected constellation) → v8 (identity/organic/weighted) → v9 (spread/uncrowded).
Live neighbour data was wired via /api/cognition/neighbours, and the unit-face became the first live-composed gallery piece.
A deterministic voice transform DNA.voiceFromDigest was added so live units never render as raw filename+digest dumps.
The real Vi V asset was located, copied into ui/assets, and mounted as the corner hub with a 5-verb socket set.
Increment 2 remains pending on the projection-side DOM-mount seam and cross-origin/CORS hosting details.
standouts: The live-compose rung is now a real, navigable gallery piece rather than only a developer harness.
One renderer, many sources was demonstrated by sight for both corpus units and authored story nodes.
The projection→gallery integration is explicitly meet-at-the-address and file-disjoint.
The file documents a full fabrication/recovery arc around the V asset and the lesson against fabricating owned assets.
conventions: Uses evidence-class labels VERIFIED / CHARACTERIZED / KNOWN.
Heavy use of star bullets to flag key findings.
Cites local screenshot paths as verification authority.
Logs self-corrections and fabrication recovery explicitly.
Uses version/raise numbering (v3–v9) and lock numbering (lock 1/2/3) as internal shorthand.
Maintains a neutral, non-ranking stance and DNA-vs-projection ownership boundaries.
concerns: The dense, rapidly iterated narrative may be hard for a new reader to parse without prior context.
Many verification claims rely on local screenshot paths and session state not present in the repository.
Version numbers, lock numbers, and raise labels are internal shorthand not defined in the file.
The integration still depends on an unresolved projection-side DOM-mount seam and same-origin/CORS assumptions.
The fabrication/recovery episode indicates a prior near-miss that required correction.
notes: A self-aware, evidence-annotated build diary that tracks how the gallery's phone-archetype UI, live unit renderer, and projection drill-in seam converged through v3–v9.
questions: What are the exact semantics of lock 1, lock 2, and lock 3?
Where is the V_ROW organism definition and the final DOM-mount seam documented?
What is the canonical repository path for the copied V SVG asset?
What does the 'kimi era-clone fan-out' mean operationally and who sequences it?
How is ownership divided between DNA.renderUnit and DNA.story over the presentation layer?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it acts as an interpretive ledger layer, though it reads more as a running build/feedback diary than a static, atemporal ledger entry.
apparent_themes: Verify by sight, not by assertion.
DNA owns the look; projection owns the selection.
Honest self-correction and recovery from mistakes.
Iterative raise/feedback loops toward top-tier visual quality.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared, evidence-based picture of the gallery's current state and the path to live-composed, projection-linked pieces, while documenting ownership boundaries and the iterative visual refinement of the unit renderer.
novelty: It is unusual as a ledger entry because it combines architectural findings, manifest inventory, event-contract, versioned visual iteration, fabrication recovery, and real asset integration in a single narrative file.
contribution: It contributes an evidence-classed interpretive record that preserves the phone-archetype specification, the 49-piece inventory, the projection:select→renderGallery(address) seam contract, the one-renderer-many-sources adapter pattern, the deterministic voice-transform rules, and the V socket vocabulary for fusion.
summary_for_embedding: GALLERY_FULL_PICTURE.md is an evidence-classed interpretive assessment of the gallery front-interface, cataloguing its phone-archetype UI, 49-piece mockup inventory, live-composed unit renderer iterations v3–v9, projection drill-in integration seam, and the recovered real-V asset/socket design.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to consolidate a solo, DNA-territory understanding of the gallery's present state and the first-slice build path toward live-composed, projection-linked gallery pieces.
